name: CCI Zero-Cross T3 Trend Momentum
edge_hypothesis: CCI Chop Fade (PF 1.255, ACCEPT, all-regime) proved CCI works for mean-reversion, but used CCI as a ZONE signal (CCI > 100). MACDh zero-cross is the most proven cross-based entry pattern (9 MACD ACCEPTs, best PF 1.712). CCI zero-line cross (CCI_20_0.015 crossing 0 from below = momentum turning from below-average to above-average) is structurally identical to MACDh zero-cross but based on DIFFERENT math: CCI measures deviation from statistical mean of price, while MACD measures convergence/divergence of exponential MAs. If CCI zero-cross works similarly to MACDh zero-cross, it confirms that zero-line momentum flips are a GENERAL entry mechanism independent of indicator construction. T3_10_0.7 provides trend direction — the SMOOTHEST available moving average (triple exponential smoothing with volume factor 0.7). Never tested as a standalone baseline. Hypothesis: CCI cross + T3 smoothness produces cleaner entries than CCI zone + CHOP gate (the prior ACCEPT), because cross-based signals have proven higher-PF outcomes than zone-based across the system.

variants.name: cci_t3_zero_v1_8to1
variants.description: Core thesis: T3 trend direction + CCI zero-line cross. 8:1 R:R. Tests CCI as a cross signal (vs zone signal in CCI Chop Fade ACCEPT). T3 provides the smoothest possible trend filter for direction alignment.

components.notes: T3 Moving Average (triple exponential smoothing, vfactor 0.7). Smoothest available MA. First standalone baseline test of T3.
CCI zero-line cross — momentum flip from below-average to above-average. Cross-based signal (vs zone-based in CCI Chop Fade). Tests if CCI cross outperforms CCI zone.
ATR-based position sizing and R:R framework.

variants.name: cci_t3_zero_v2_tight
variants.description: Champion-style tight stop: 1.0 ATR stop, 10 ATR TP (10:1). If CCI zero-cross entries are high-conviction (they catch the exact momentum flip), tighter stops should work because winners don't dip back past 1.0 ATR. Same principle that made Vortex v3a the champion.

components.notes: T3 trend direction — smoothest MA baseline.
CCI zero-cross. Champion-style tight stop geometry.

variants.name: cci_t3_zero_v3_wide_tp
variants.description: Extended target test: 1.5 ATR stop, 15 ATR TP (10:1). If T3's smooth trend read holds positions in-trend longer without whipsaw, wider TP should capture extended moves. Tests whether T3's low-lag smoothing enables more patient profit taking than EMA-based strategies.

components.notes: T3 smooth trend — tests if smoothing enables wider TP.
Extended 15x ATR TP for trend continuation capture.

indicator_roles.baseline: T3_10_0.7 — Triple exponential smoothing MA (vfactor 0.7). Smoothest available moving average. First standalone baseline test.
indicator_roles.confirmation: CCI_20_0.015 — Commodity Channel Index. Zero-line cross as momentum flip signal. Tests CCI as cross-based (vs zone-based in CCI Chop Fade ACCEPT).
indicator_roles.volume_volatility: ATR_14 — Position sizing and R:R framework.
